methods.name: LiTAMIN2
methods.summary: KL-divergence ICP with aggressive point reduction for fast scan-to-map registration.

methods.name: GICP
methods.summary: Plane-to-plane ICP with local covariance modeling and Mahalanobis distance.

methods.name: Voxel-GICP
methods.summary: Voxel-level GICP acceleration for compact covariance-aware registration.

methods.name: VGICP-SLAM
methods.summary: Voxel-GICP front-end with Scan Context loop candidates and a lightweight pose graph.

methods.name: small_gicp
methods.summary: Compact GICP with voxel downsampling and capped correspondences.

methods.name: NDT
methods.summary: Normal Distributions Transform style registration with voxel Gaussian models.

methods.name: KISS-ICP
methods.summary: KISS-ICP style odometry with voxel hashing and robust ICP updates.

methods.name: GenZ-ICP
methods.summary: GenZ-ICP style odometry blending point-to-plane and point-to-point with adaptive weighting for degeneracy robustness.

methods.name: RKO-LIO
methods.summary: RKO-LIO style scan-to-map odometry using an IMU gyro rotation prior, with constant-velocity fallback when no IMU is present.

methods.name: FR-LIO
methods.summary: FR-LIO style RC-Vox scan-to-map LIO with adaptive sub-frames, IMU preintegration prior, and simplified backward ESKS smoothing.

methods.name: Karto-Matcher
methods.summary: Map-based correlative scan matching for 2D laser odometry using a rolling local occupancy map and multi-resolution distance-transform scoring (Olson ICRA 2009 / Karto-style extension).

methods.name: MbICP
methods.summary: Metric-based ICP 2D laser odometry that uses a configuration-space distance to account for translation and rotation together during correspondence search and least-squares alignment (Minguez, Lamiraux & Montesano, ICRA 2005 / T-RO 2006).

methods.name: IDC
methods.summary: Iterative Dual Correspondence 2D laser odometry that fuses closest-point translation with matching-range rotation each ICP iteration (Lu & Milios, 1997).

methods.name: NDT-2D
methods.summary: 2D Normal Distributions Transform laser odometry that registers scans against a grid of per-cell Gaussians without explicit point correspondences (Biber & Straßer, IROS 2003).

methods.name: PSM
methods.summary: Polar scan matching 2D laser odometry that aligns scans by correlating range profiles in the sensor polar frame (Hähnel & Burgard PSM, ICRA 2003).

methods.name: Kinematic-ICP
methods.summary: Kinematic-ICP 2D laser odometry fusing unicycle wheel odometry with point-to-line ICP (Guadagnino ICRA 2025). A point-style robot-frame local map is implemented but opt-in: it rescues some long train windows while breaking the MIT val and fr079 long windows, with no safe shared configuration.

methods.name: CSM
methods.summary: Multi-resolution correlative scan matching for 2D laser odometry using occupancy-grid pose search (Olson ICRA 2009).

methods.name: PL-ICP
methods.summary: PL-ICP 2D laser scan matching using point-to-line metric and iterative Gauss-Newton SE(2) alignment (Censi IROS 2008).

methods.name: RF2O
methods.summary: RF2O range-flow 2D laser odometry using dense scan gradients and coarse-to-fine robust least squares (ICRA 2016). A robot-frame local map is implemented but opt-in: its min-range polar reference helps short validation windows and degrades every long Bonn train window.

methods.name: PG-LIO
methods.summary: PG-LIO style photometric-geometric LIO fusing NCC range-image patches with point-to-plane ICP and IMU priors on degenerate directions.

methods.name: A-LOAM
methods.summary: Curvature features with a three-stage odom-to-map LOAM pipeline.

methods.name: F-LOAM
methods.summary: Lightweight LOAM pipeline with input thinning and sparse map updates.

methods.name: ISC-LOAM
methods.summary: Intensity descriptor LOAM with F-LOAM and GICP loop graph components.

methods.name: LOAM Livox
methods.summary: LOAM adaptation for solid-state LiDAR using azimuth-sector pseudo scan lines.

methods.name: LeGO-LOAM
methods.summary: Ground-aware feature extraction for vehicle-oriented LOAM.

methods.name: MULLS
methods.summary: Multi-metric scan-to-map alignment with edge, plane, and point residuals.

methods.name: BALM2
methods.summary: Local bundle adjustment over recent keyframes with line and plane residuals.

methods.name: SuMa
methods.summary: Dense surfel odometry from range-image maps.

methods.name: DLO
methods.summary: Direct keyframe odometry that aligns dense scans to a local GICP map.

methods.name: HDL Graph SLAM
methods.summary: NDT front-end with floor priors, Scan Context, and GICP loop closures.

methods.name: CT-ICP
methods.summary: Continuous-time registration with two poses per frame and SLERP compensation.

methods.name: X-ICP
methods.summary: Constrained ICP using Hessian SVD to classify observable directions.

methods.name: RELEAD
methods.summary: Constrained ESIKF with projection-based suppression along degenerate directions.

methods.name: CT-ICP + RELEAD
methods.summary: Continuous-time CT-ICP interpolation combined with RELEAD degeneracy handling.

methods.name: IMU Preintegration
methods.summary: SO(3) preintegration with first-order bias correction for inertial pipelines.

methods.name: CT-LIO
methods.summary: Lightweight LIO combining CT-ICP interpolation with IMU preintegration constraints.

methods.name: DLIO
methods.summary: DLIO-style direct LiDAR-inertial odometry built on dense registration and preintegration.

methods.name: LINS
methods.summary: Iterated-filter LiDAR-inertial estimator with point-to-plane updates.

methods.name: Point-LIO
methods.summary: Direct LIO with raw-point planarity correspondences and iterated filtering.

methods.name: CLINS
methods.summary: Sequence-pipeline version of CT-LIO style continuous-time registration.

methods.name: LIO-SAM
methods.summary: Pose graph with A-LOAM front-end, Scan Context, GICP, and IMU priors.

methods.name: FAST-LIO2
methods.summary: Direct LIO with raw-point scan-to-map alignment and IMU prediction.

methods.name: FAST-LIO-SLAM
methods.summary: FAST-LIO2 front-end with Scan Context and GICP loop-closure graph SLAM.

methods.name: CUBE-LIO Repro
methods.summary: Cubemap intensity and gradient front-end reproduction for CUBE-LIO style experiments.

methods.name: VILENS
methods.summary: Visual-lidar-inertial smoother with Point-LIO style local maps and reprojection fusion.

methods.name: LVI-SAM
methods.summary: Visual-lidar-inertial SLAM built on a LIO-SAM style pose graph.

methods.name: VINS-Fusion
methods.summary: Compact visual-inertial odometry with landmark reprojection and preintegration.

methods.name: OKVIS
methods.summary: Fixed-window visual-inertial odometry with landmark reprojection factors.

methods.name: ORB-SLAM3
methods.summary: Visual-inertial SLAM with keyframe graph and overlap-based loop closure.

methods.name: FAST-LIVO2
methods.summary: Local visual-lidar-inertial odometry built on FAST-LIO2 poses and reprojection residuals.

methods.name: R2LIVE
methods.summary: Visual-lidar-inertial SLAM combining FAST-LIO2 odometry and visual landmark factors.

methods.name: Scan Context
methods.summary: Polar ring-sector descriptors with yaw-shift search for place recognition.

methods.name: Adaptive-ICP
methods.summary: Reliable-initial-pose adaptive ICP: density-filtered coarse pose vs constant-velocity prediction, motion-state-weighted sigma_th, robust point-to-plane (arXiv:2509.22058).

methods.name: D2-LIO
methods.summary: Adaptive per-point outlier threshold + scan-to-submap IMU prior + degeneracy-aware hybrid weighting (arXiv:2508.14355).

methods.name: CT-VoxelMap
methods.summary: Probabilistic adaptive voxel map (running mu/Sigma), point-uncertainty propagation, Mahalanobis hybrid point-to-plane/point-to-distribution (arXiv:2604.03747).

methods.name: R-VoxelMap
methods.summary: Recursive octree plane fitting with outlier detect-and-reuse, plane-variance-weighted point-to-plane, and low-correspondence scan-to-scan recovery for the strict KITTI evaluator (arXiv:2601.12377).

methods.name: DegenSense
methods.summary: Real-time degeneracy factor (Hessian condition number) + median+k*MAD outlier detection + IMU/LiDAR pose fusion (arXiv:2412.07513).

methods.name: Vibration-LIO
methods.summary: Post-undistortion point covariance with IMU-MAD vibration intensity, Mahalanobis-weighted point-to-plane (arXiv:2507.04311).

methods.name: BIEVR-LIO
methods.summary: Bump image (per-voxel oriented height grid); image gradients add in-plane constraints to point-to-plane residual (arXiv:2604.14421).

methods.name: UA-LIO
methods.summary: Gaussian voxel map, distribution-to-distribution uncertainty weighting, ground-plane z-constraint (IEEE TIM 2025).

methods.name: DTD
methods.summary: Density Triangle Descriptor: BEV density keypoints + rotation/translation-invariant triangle descriptor with entropy, hash voting + SVD verification (PMC 2025).

methods.name: DAMM-LOAM
methods.summary: Degeneracy-aware multi-metric LiDAR odometry: range-image normal-class weighting (ground/wall/edge) + Hessian-eigenvalue degeneracy boost (arXiv:2510.13287).

methods.name: LODESTAR
methods.summary: Degeneracy-aware LiDAR odometry: condition-number Schmidt-Kalman anchoring of degenerate directions to a sliding-window constant-velocity prediction (DA-ASKF) + localizability-based data exploitation/pruning (DA-DE) (arXiv:2511.09142).

methods.name: Terrain-RBF-LIO
methods.summary: Terrain-aware LiDAR odometry: Gaussian-RBF terrain height field updated by RLS, used as a vehicle-height-above-terrain soft prior to suppress z-drift (legged-wheel contact constraint reinterpreted for the car platform) (arXiv:2509.26222).

methods.name: LiDAR-IBA
methods.summary: Consistency-improved LiDAR bundle adjustment: stereographic plane-normal parameterization (no over-parameterization/ground-plane singularity) + sliding-window plane BA with FEJ (arXiv:2602.06380).

methods.name: DALI-SLAM
methods.summary: Dual-spline motion distortion correction (continuous-time spline deskew) + degeneracy-aware solution remapping over the registration Hessian (ISPRS J. P&RS 221, 2025).

methods.name: Intensity-Flow
methods.summary: Intensity-enhanced LiDAR odometry: RMS geometric gradient-flow sampling (redundancy-minimizing, distribution-preserving) + intensity-geometry fused point-to-plane matching (Measurement, 2026).

methods.name: I-LOAM
methods.summary: Intensity Enhanced LOAM (UR 2020): LOAM odometry+mapping with reflectance injected into scan-to-scan correspondence (geometry + lambda*delta-intensity^2) and residual weighting (w=exp(-delta-intensity^2/2sigma^2)), plus aloam LaserMapping. KITTI seq07 ~0.58% drift with dense profile.

methods.name: PL-LOAM
methods.summary: PL-LOAM (ICRA 2020): LiDAR-monocular point+line visual odometry with robust LiDAR depth extraction and depth-prior point-line bundle adjustment plus scale correction. KITTI Odometry eval uses LiDAR-intensity pseudo-images with LiDAR depth priors because RGB is absent from the odometry benchmark.

methods.name: InTEn-LOAM
methods.summary: InTEn-LOAM (RS 2022/23): cylindrical range+intensity FEF, B-spline intensity registration, simplified TVF/DOR, and scan-to-map mapping. KITTI post-fix ablation (2026-07-03) is high-drift / honest negative.

methods.name: MCGICP-LO
methods.summary: MCGICP (Servos & Waslander, RAS 2017): multi-channel GICP that integrates LiDAR intensity into 4D point covariances and Mahalanobis scan-to-map registration. No author code.

methods.name: ICPSC-LO
methods.summary: ICPSC (Zhang et al., JAG 2023): cylindrical intensity shape-context descriptor with adaptive geometry/intensity fusion for scan-to-map odometry. Loop closure deferred in this port.

methods.name: VLOM
methods.summary: Scale-correction VLOM (arXiv:2304.08978): loosely coupled monocular VO with LiDAR depth scale correction and visual bootstrapping for A-LOAM odometry+mapping. KITTI Odometry eval keeps A-LOAM mapping and scale correction active but disables visual bootstrap on LiDAR-intensity pseudo-images because RGB is absent from the odometry benchmark.

methods.name: OdoNet
methods.summary: OdoNet (IEEE Sensors J. 2022, arXiv:2109.03091): 1D-CNN pseudo-odometer regresses forward speed from a 50×6 IMU window; fused with strapdown INS, NHC, and ZUPT for vehicle dead reckoning. Trained on KITTI Raw OXTS; requires imu.csv at eval time.

methods.name: NHC-Net
methods.summary: NHC-Net/VMSC (GPS Solutions 2023): CNN classifies vehicle motion state and regresses lateral/vertical velocity for adaptive non-holonomic constraints on strapdown INS dead reckoning. Trained on KITTI Raw OXTS; requires imu.csv at eval time.

methods.name: NN-ZUPT
methods.summary: NN-ZUPT (Meas. Sci. Technol. 2023): 1D-CNN detects zero-velocity intervals from a 50×6 IMU window; fused with strapdown INS, NHC, and velocity-reset ZUPT. Trained on KITTI Raw OXTS; requires imu.csv at eval time.

methods.name: IMU Dead Reckoning
methods.summary: Pure strapdown IMU-only dead reckoning: static-window gyro-bias/gravity init, midpoint gyro integration, and double integration of specific force, with no LiDAR, NHC, or ZUPT unless opted in. The unaided lower-bound reference for the LIO/OdoNet/NHC-Net/NN-ZUPT family; not a paper reimplementation.

methods.name: SVN-ICP
methods.summary: Uncertainty-aware ICP LiDAR odometry: a particle posterior refined by Stein Variational Newton on SE(3) yields both a point-to-plane registration and a pose covariance without explicit noise modelling (arXiv:2509.08069).

methods.name: PCR-DAT
methods.summary: Point cloud registration that switches per point between a Gauss-distribution (rich-feature) factor and a point-to-plane distance (sparse-feature) factor, fused in one Gauss-Newton system to avoid local optima in mixed-density scenes (Intelligent Service Robotics 2024, DOI:10.1007/s11370-024-00517-6).

methods.name: Small-but-Mighty
methods.summary: Lightweight LiDAR-odometry feature enhancement: stability-aware feature selection from the statistical distribution of local smoothness plus a contribution-weighted point-to-plane/point-to-line optimization that emphasizes stable features and suppresses low-quality residuals (Remote Sensing 2025, 17(15):2656).

methods.name: M-GCLO
methods.summary: Multiple-ground-constrained LiDAR odometry: classifies points into ground/non-ground, applies multiple-ground-plane point-to-plane constraints for vertical accuracy and non-ground point-to-distribution (NDT) matching, with per-point range-uncertainty residual weighting (ISPRS Annals X-1-2024, 283-290).

methods.name: Quadric-LO
methods.summary: Quadric-surface scene representation for LiDAR odometry: fits local patches to implicit quadric functions q=xAx+bx+c (planes are the degenerate case) and minimizes point-to-quadric Taubin-distance residuals, capturing curved structures more faithfully than planes (Xia et al., IEEE RA-L 2023, arXiv:2304.14190).

methods.name: DiLO
methods.summary: Direct LiDAR odometry on spherical range images: frame-to-keyframe alignment via bounded projective data association (no nearest-neighbor search) with image-based point-to-plane residuals. Fast (~65 FPS) and stable after 1px lookup gating, but still less accurate than persistent scan-to-map local-map methods (Han et al., ETRI Journal 2021).

methods.name: ELO
methods.summary: Efficient LiDAR odometry using non-ground spherical range-image projective association fused with a ground bird's-eye-view map, range-adaptive PCA normals, and memory-efficient SRI/BEV model updates (Zheng & Zhu, IEEE RA-L 2021, arXiv:2104.10879).

methods.name: ID-LIO
methods.summary: Indexed-point dynamic LiDAR-inertial odometry: pseudo-occupancy range-image checks mark foreground/disappearing dynamic points, indexed map points carry confidence/missing age, and delayed removal plus dynamic residual weights protect scan-to-map optimization; IMU gyro preintegration is used as a rotation prior when synchronized imu.csv is present (Wu & Wang, Sensors 2023).

methods.name: RF-LIO
methods.summary: Removal-first LiDAR-inertial odometry: adaptive multi-resolution range images reject likely foreground dynamic points before scan-to-map optimization, then an indexed dynamic map backend handles delayed removal and IMU gyro-prior support; KITTI Odometry runs use constant-velocity fallback because imu.csv is absent (Qian et al., IROS 2021 / arXiv:2206.09463).

methods.name: TC-LVGF
methods.summary: Tightly coupled LiDAR-visual geometric-feature odometry: LiDAR linear features and pseudo-visual range-image line segments are associated in a spherical fusion frame, then fused point-to-line and direction residuals augment scan-to-map point-to-plane optimization with LiDAR fallback when visual line support is sparse (Cao et al., ROBIO 2023 / arXiv:2307.07763).

methods.name: OPL-LVIO
methods.summary: Optimized point-line LiDAR-visual-inertial odometry: range-image point and line proxies stand in for visual features on KITTI PCD, LiDAR depth correlation supplies metric landmarks, and scan-to-map plane plus visual point residuals are balanced by Helmert-style variance component weighting; pseudo-line residuals are extracted but disabled in the dense profile after a short-window ablation (He et al., Remote Sensing 2022).

methods.name: V-LOAM2015
methods.summary: Visual-LiDAR odometry and mapping adapter: range-image visual point proxies provide a conservative bootstrap/prior, then LiDAR scan-to-map point-to-plane refinement estimates the final pose; the camera VO, global mapping, and loop closure stages are out of scope for the KITTI PCD harness (Zhang and Singh, ICRA 2015).

methods.name: TC-VLO
methods.summary: Tightly coupled vision-LiDAR odometry adapter: sparse range-image visual point proxies, pseudo-visual line support, and LiDAR scan-to-map plane constraints are jointly optimized through point, line, and plane residuals, with LiDAR fallback when visual support is sparse (Seo and Chou, IV 2019).

methods.name: AD-VLO
methods.summary: Accurate direct visual-laser odometry adapter: occlusion-aware range-jump gating and plane-emphasized scan-to-map residuals approximate the paper's direct visual-laser alignment and explicit plane handling on Velodyne-only KITTI PCD data (Huang, Xiao, and Stachniss, ICRA 2019).

methods.name: TC-MVLO
methods.summary: Tightly coupled monocular visual-LiDAR odometry adapter: range-image visual point proxies and fused visual/LiDAR line support augment LiDAR scan-to-map constraints with Helmert-style point/line residual balancing; loop closure is out of scope in the KITTI PCD port (Meng, Ye, and Lin, Intelligent Service Robotics 2022).

methods.name: NHC-LIO
methods.summary: Vehicle LiDAR odometry with a nonholonomic-constraint (NHC) factor: the body-frame lateral and vertical translation velocity is constrained to ~0, added as a soft factor to scan-to-map point-to-plane with a yaw-rate-adaptive weight (the paper's IMU/CNN-predicted reliable NHC is approximated by motion state on IMU-free KITTI) (Chen et al., IEEE Sensors Journal 2023).

methods.name: Student-T-LO
methods.summary: Heavy-tail robust LiDAR odometry: scan-to-map point-to-plane residuals are modeled with a Student's-t distribution and solved by IRLS with per-correspondence weight w=(nu+1)/(nu+(r/sigma)^2), where the scale sigma is updated by EM each iteration (sigma^2=sum w r^2 / N). The variable degree-of-freedom nu generalizes the Cauchy kernel (nu=1) and approaches Gaussian as nu grows (2024 Student's-t point-cloud registration, PMC11314997).

methods.name: Spectral-LO
methods.summary: Frequency-domain LiDAR odometry with no ICP: each scan is rasterized to a bird's-eye-view density image, the relative yaw is recovered by Fourier-Mellin log-polar phase correlation on the translation-invariant magnitude spectrum, and the relative translation by phase-only correlation (POC) of the de-rotated BEV images. Estimates a planar 3-DoF (x, y, yaw) motion with z held (SROM-style phase-correlation matching, arXiv:2005.02042).

methods.name: GMM-LO
methods.summary: Gaussian-mixture / EM soft-assignment LiDAR odometry. Unlike hard nearest-neighbor ICP or hard voxel assignment (NDT), each source point is softly associated to its neighboring map points by Gaussian responsibilities gamma_ij with a uniform-outlier term (E-step); the M-step minimizes a soft-centroid point-to-plane cost, and sigma is annealed from coarse to fine (deterministic annealing) for a wide-to-sharp basin (compact single-scale reduction of HGMR's tree of Gaussian mixtures, Eckart et al. 2018, arXiv:1807.02587).

methods.name: GNC-LO
methods.summary: Graduated-Non-Convexity (GNC) robust point-to-plane LiDAR odometry. Unlike fixed M-estimator kernels (Cauchy/Student-t) or EM scale annealing, GNC anneals the non-convexity of the surrogate itself: a Truncated-Least-Squares cost is solved by weighted least squares while the control parameter mu is continued from a near-convex value (wide basin) toward the non-convex TLS, ending in hard inlier/outlier rejection (Yang, Antonante, Tzoumas, Carlone, IEEE RA-L 2020, arXiv:1909.08605).

methods.name: MCC-LO
methods.summary: Maximum-Correntropy-Criterion (MCC) robust point-to-plane LiDAR odometry. Instead of minimizing squared error, it maximizes the correntropy V=sum exp(-r^2/2 sigma^2); half-quadratic optimization yields an IRLS Welsch/Gaussian weight w=exp(-r^2/2 sigma^2), and the kernel bandwidth sigma is adapted per iteration from the residual distribution via the Silverman rule. An information-theoretic robust family distinct from M-estimator heavy tails, GNC continuation, and GMM soft assignment (He et al., PLOS ONE 2018, doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0197542; Pattern Recognition 2019).

methods.name: IMLS-SLAM
methods.summary: Scan-to-model LiDAR odometry that matches the new scan against an Implicit Moving Least Squares (IMLS) surface defined by the oriented points of past scans: I^P(x)=sum W_i (x-p_i).n_i / sum W_i with Gaussian weights W_i=exp(-||x-p_i||^2/h^2). Each sampled point is registered to this smooth higher-order implicit surface (point-to-implicit-surface) by Gauss-Newton, and an observability-based sampling selects the points that best constrain the 6-DoF pose. A distinct geometric family from single-nearest-plane point-to-plane and from voxel-surfel maps (Deschaud, ICRA 2018, arXiv:1802.08633; author released CT-ICP but not IMLS-SLAM).

methods.name: Mesh-LOAM
methods.summary: Mesh-based scan-to-model LiDAR odometry: every scanned point scatters an IMLS-weighted signed-distance increment into a voxel-hash SDF map (the O(N) 'passive voxel' scheme with hybrid distance+normal weights), an explicit triangle mesh is extracted incrementally per voxel block from the SDF zero set, and the next scan is registered point-to-mesh with Gauss-Newton under a strict normal cosine-similarity correspondence gate (|cos|>0.98). A distinct map representation family from surfels (SuMa), implicit-surface matching (IMLS-SLAM), and voxel plane maps (Zhu, Zheng & Zhu, IEEE T-IV 2024, arXiv:2312.15630; official repo is README-only, no code).

methods.name: TrICP-LO
methods.summary: Trimmed / Fractional ICP (Least Trimmed Squares) point-to-plane LiDAR odometry. Unlike M-estimators (Student-T/Cauchy/MCC) and GNC that down-weight residuals by magnitude, TrICP sorts residuals by rank and keeps only the best xi fraction in the least-squares solve (a high-breakdown LTS estimator), and estimates the overlap ratio xi automatically by minimizing the Fractional RMSD FRMSD(xi)=sqrt(e(xi))/xi^lambda, explicitly rejecting the non-overlapping part (newly appearing / disappearing / dynamic points) between consecutive scans (Chetverikov et al., ICPR 2002 / IVC 2005; Phillips et al., 3DIM 2007).

methods.name: KC-LO
methods.summary: Correspondence-free Kernel Correlation LiDAR odometry. Instead of taking nearest-neighbour correspondences (ICP), latent assignments (GMM-EM), or voxel Gaussians (NDT), it treats the two clouds as kernel density estimates and maximizes their kernel correlation C=sum_i sum_j exp(-||T x_i - y_j||^2 / 2 sigma^2), equivalent to minimizing the Renyi quadratic entropy of the combined density. Each scan point is pulled toward the affinity-weighted mean of nearby map points (a soft point-to-point step), with coarse-to-fine sigma annealing for a wide basin of convergence (Tsin & Kanade, ECCV 2004).

starter_tracks.title: Quick Smoke
starter_tracks.note: Smallest path from clone to comparable LiDAR numbers.

starter_tracks.label: Accuracy First
starter_tracks.title: Accuracy First
starter_tracks.note: Scan-to-map methods that currently anchor the Istanbul benchmark snapshot.

starter_tracks.label: Fusion
starter_tracks.title: LiDAR-Inertial Fusion
starter_tracks.note: Start here when IMU data is available and timing matters.

starter_tracks.label: Degeneracy
starter_tracks.title: Degeneracy-Aware Stack
starter_tracks.note: Methods aimed at tunnels, corridors, and weak geometric constraints.
